title: Ivan Yaaro Lyrics Minnale

Verenna Verenna Vendum
orumurai sonnaal podhum

kaal midhiyaai vaipene

uyirayum naan tharuvene

oh ....
oh mounam mounam

Ivan yaaro Ivan yaaro
vandhadhu edharkkaaga
sirikindraan rasikindraan
enakke enakkaaga

ennaachu enake
theriyavillai

maattik kondaen

idhu pudikkiradha

Thottathil ulla
thottathil ulla
pookkal ellaame
vanna pookkal ellaame.

thalayai thiruppi
paarkkumaanaal

unaithaane.

nenje nenje unnai
ullae vaithadhu yaaru

nee varum paadhi
engum, en iru
ullangai thaangum ..

kaalgalin koluse

naan andha idhathil

yaeno yaeno ennai
paarka seithaai unnai

naan unnai kaanathaanaa
ugamthorum
kaaththukidanthaenaa..

Ivan yaaro Ivan yaaro
vandhadhu edharkkaaga
sirikindraan rasikindraan
hmm mmm hmm mmm.

naanthaane naanthaane

ennachu enakke

orey oru murai solli vidu

seo.title: Ivan Yaaro Lyrics Minnale
seo.description: Full Ivan Yaaro songs lyrics, Minnale Songs Lyrics. sharable lyrics snippets, Lyrics: Thamarai, Music: Harrish Jeyaraj, Singer(s): Harini, Unnikrishnan.
seo.keywords: Thamarai, Minnale, Harrish Jeyaraj, Harini, Unnikrishnan, Ivan Yaaro full lyrics, Minnale songs lyrics, Ivan Yaaro lyrics snippets, Ivan Yaaro WhatsApp status lyrics snippets, share Ivan Yaaro lyrics snippets with friends, Tamil lyrics, Tamil songs

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Ivan Yaaro?

The song Ivan Yaaro is a romantic melody that explores themes of love and longing.

Who are the singers of Ivan Yaaro?

The song is beautifully sung by Harini and Unnikrishnan.

Who composed the music for Minnale?

The music for Minnale was composed by Harrish Jeyaraj.

What is the release year of Minnale?

The movie Minnale was released in the year 2001.

What role does the song play in the movie?

The song Ivan Yaaro captures a moment of introspection and emotional depth in the film, highlighting the protagonist's feelings.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the movie Minnale, the song Ivan Yaaro serves as a poignant expression of love and introspection. Sung by Harini and Unnikrishnan, the melody beautifully conveys the emotions of longing and affection. Thamarai's lyrics add depth to the song, making it a memorable romantic piece. Harrish Jeyaraj's composition enhances the soothing and melancholic mood, creating an unforgettable musical experience.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Experience the enchanting melody of Ivan Yaaro from the movie Minnale, where Harini and Unnikrishnan's soulful voices blend seamlessly with Harrish Jeyaraj's mesmerizing music. This romantic duet, penned by Thamarai, captures the essence of love and longing. Dive into the soothing rhythms and let the music transport you to a world of emotions.
stanzaMeanings: What do I need? If you say it once, it's enough. I will make the moon touch the ground.
No need to say anything, your eyes are enough. Without questions, I will give my life.
Oh... oh silence, silence, silence, silence. Why do I need this? I am doing, I am doing.
Who is he? Who is he? Why did he come? He is smiling, enjoying, just for me.
What happened? I don't know. My breath's fever is not reducing.
What is this? What is this? How did I get caught like this?
Do I like this or not? Who should I ask and tell?
Who is he?
In the garden, in the garden, all the flowers are colorful, all the flowers.
If you turn your head to look, it was me who called you, it was me who called you.
Heart, heart, who kept you inside?
Wherever you come halfway, my two palms will hold...
Who is he?
The anklets on my feet, the anklets on my feet, are getting angry with you, getting angry with you.
I was eager to shine in that place, you came and ruined it, sinner, you came and ruined it.
Why, why did you make me see you?
I waited every moment without seeing you.
Who is he? Who is he? Why did he come? He is smiling, enjoying, hmm mmm hmm mmm.
It was me, it was me who came for you, smiling, enjoying, just for you.
What happened? I don't know. My breath's fever is not reducing.
What is this? What is this? Talk to me.
Do you like me or not? Tell me once, just once, tell me, tell me, tell me, tell me.
